name: Claude Opus 4.8
archDisplay: Dense transformer, architecture undisclosed; adaptive thinking controlled by a runtime effort parameter (low/medium/high/xhigh/max)
note: Model id claude-opus-4-8. Introduced 'dynamic workflows' in Claude Code and a 'fast mode' (2.5x speed) priced at $10/$50 per MTok, three times cheaper than prior fast-mode pricing. Regular (non-fast) pricing unchanged from Opus 4.7 at $5/$25. Best-in-class computer-use/browser-agent model at launch (84% on Online-Mind2Web, self-reported).

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified, attributed to Anthropic's system card Table 8.1.A.
GPQA Diamond; roughly flat vs Opus 4.7 (94.2%), within saturation noise.
Humanity's Last Exam, no-tools. With-tools scored 57.9% (not separately filed).
PROPOSED NEW SLUG: 'osworld-verified' has no existing match in the valid slug list. Pass@1 on the OSWorld-Verified computer-use benchmark.

PROPOSED NEW SLUG distinct from base 'gdpval': GDPval-AA v2 is Anthropic's harder/updated economically-valuable-work eval, scored on a 0-2000 scale, not the same test as the original GDPval and should not be filed under the base slug.
Single-agent configuration, attributed to Anthropic's official system card per multiple corroborating sources; multi-agent harness scored 88.5% per the same source.
Independent benchmark run by Contra Collective (their own testing, not an Anthropic claim), confirmed via direct fetch.
FrontierMath v2 Tiers 1-3, attributed to the independent Epoch AI FrontierMath v2 leaderboard.
FrontierMath v2 Tier 4, attributed to the independent Epoch AI FrontierMath v2 leaderboard (claude-opus-4-8_max).

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Claude Code harness, Max reasoning effort: Pass rate 27.0% / Score 45.2% (rank 9 overall). Using the Score component per this corpus's established convention for this benchmark (see bytedance/seed-2-1-pro.json, openai/gpt-5-6.json). Mirrored from the official leaderboard at agents-last-exam.org/leaderboard (Berkeley RDI); catalog model has no effort-variant split, so the best-scoring configuration (Max) is used as the headline figure.

USAMO 2026, confirmed via direct fetch of datacamp.com/blog/claude-opus-4-8. Independently corroborated (same 96.7% figure) via direct fetches of llm-stats.com/blog/research/claude-opus-4-8-launch and digitalapplied.com/blog/claude-opus-4-8-release-dynamic-workflows-2026, both explicitly citing the Anthropic Claude Opus 4.8 System Card. Represents a 27.4pp jump over Opus 4.7's 69.3%, described across sources as the largest single-cycle math gain in the Opus line. Not currently tracked in this corpus for this model.

Extracted from the page's embedded CSS bar-width value (87.819%); JS count-up animation shows 0.0% in a plain static fetch, so read directly from HTML source. Cross-validated by reproducing this model's known SWE-bench score (88.6%) via the same method.
Extracted from the page's embedded CSS bar-width value (86.59%), same method/validation as the livecodebench entry.

FrontierBench v0.1 (74 tasks, 7 domains), Claude Code harness, max effort: resolution rate 21.1% ±1.6%, rank 3 of 8 on the official leaderboard; 5.2B tokens, ~$5.7k run cost. Run by the FrontierBench team (Terminal-Bench/Harbor maintainers).

verificationNotes:
Release date, model id, and pricing confirmed directly from Anthropic's own announcement and platform docs (fetched). Benchmark table numbers below are relayed via a third-party writeup that explicitly cites 'Claude Opus 4.8 System Card, Table 8.1.A'.

2026-07-16: Researched the six missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu). Fetched Anthropic's official Claude Opus 4.8 System Card PDF directly (www-cdn.anthropic.com/0b4915911bb0d19eca5b5ee635c80fef830a37ea.pdf, 8,282 lines of extracted text) and searched it exhaustively: 'HumanEval' has zero occurrences anywhere in the document; standalone 'MATH' as a benchmark name has zero occurrences; 'MMLU' appears only as GMMLU (a 42-language variant, Section 8.15.1) and MMLU-Pro (used solely as a probe-question set for a chain-of-thought-controllability safety eval, Section 6.5.1) — neither is a scored capability-benchmark row. The main Table 8.1.A capability evaluation summary (the card's own headline benchmark table) does not include MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, or MMMU at all — its focus has shifted entirely to agentic/computer-use/coding evals (SWE-bench variants, BrowseComp, Terminal-Bench 2.1, OSWorld-Verified, GDPval-AA, MCP-Atlas, GraphWalks, etc). The card explicitly states (p.198, Section 8.7) that AIME 'was a popular AI benchmark last year but is now saturated,' and that USAMO 2026 (already tracked in this file at 96.7%) is its replacement in the math-olympiad track. Cross-checked two independent trackers (vals.ai, artificialanalysis.ai) and one benchmark aggregator (benchlm.ai, whose dedicated /math leaderboard shows dashes for AIME/HMMT/MATH-500 rows for this exact model) — none report MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval scores for Claude Opus 4.8, consistent with these being legitimately unpublished saturated legacy benchmarks rather than a research gap. For livecodebench and mmmu, vals.ai's independent leaderboard page for this model (fetched directly) does carry live scores: LiveCodeBench 87.82% and MMMU 86.59%, both added. (Vals.ai's page renders these via a JS count-up animation that shows 0.0% in a plain static HTML fetch; the true values were read from the embedded CSS bar-width attributes in the fetched HTML, and the method was cross-validated by confirming it reproduces this model's known SWE-bench score of exactly 88.6% via the identical extraction, matching the self-reported figure already on file.)

2026-07-17 (round 2, new-source-type sweep for mmlu/math/aime/humaneval): Checked all 6 newly-specified source types; found no credible score for any of the four gaps. Conclusion: omitted_no_evidence for all four, on top of the round-1 finding.

1. Stanford HELM (Classic, Lite, Capabilities leaderboards): Claude Opus 4.8 is absent. Corroborating evidence found independently: HELM's own GitHub releases show its last version (v0.5.16) shipped 2026-04-30, and multiple sources confirm HELM entered maintenance mode on 2026-06-01 -- Opus 4.8 launched 2026-05-28, in the ~4-week window after HELM's last active release and just before it stopped evaluating new models entirely. HELM's public model roster (per search results) tops out at Claude Opus 4.7; 4.8 was never run.

2. arXiv / Semantic Scholar baseline citations: Searched multiple query variants ("Claude Opus 4.8" MMLU arxiv, MATH-500/AIME/HumanEval comparison tables, Table 1/Table 2 baseline tables). Found papers citing older "Claude Opus 4" or "Claude Opus 4.6" with MMLU/MATH-500/AIME numbers (e.g. GLM-4.5 tech report), but nothing for the specific claude-opus-4-8 model. No paper reports a real MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval number for this exact model.

3. GitHub eval-harness run logs: `gh search code` and web search surfaced several repos referencing "claude-opus-4-8" alongside these benchmark names, but none survive scrutiny: (a) best-of-ai/best-of-ai and smfworks/aiclearinghouse-site carry unsourced wiki-style fields (mmlu_score: 92.4, humaneval_score: 92.5/93.0) with zero methodology or citation; (b) future-agi/future-agi's available_models.py contains a demonstrably hallucinated description (claims an "8K token context window" for a model whose actual window is 1M) alongside an MMLU figure, disqualifying it as a source; (c) Anakin7n/PolicyFlow hardcodes an unlabeled score tuple with a code comment admitting it came from an ad hoc "arxiv.org search," not a real eval; (d) edhaynes/eds-rules has a genuine committed run log (`coding-bench-run.log`) but it's a personal 40-of-164-problem HumanEval subset run (39/40, 98%) for a cost-routing experiment, not the standard full-set pass@1 this corpus's `humaneval` slug requires; (e) andreafreda/codecraft has real per-problem results but only 6 HumanEval/MultiPL-E tasks used for a code-readability study; (f) KxSystems/q-evaluation-harness evaluates a "q-humaneval" variant (164 KDB+/q-language problems), a different benchmark from the standard Python HumanEval despite the name. None of these are usable as the corpus's `humaneval` (standard 164-task pass@1) score.

4. Other leaderboards/aggregators: OpenRouter's Claude Opus 4.8 model page has a "Benchmarks" section present but empty of MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval data. lmcouncil.ai/benchmarks (fetched directly) lists Claude Opus 4.8 on OTIS Mock AIME 2024-25 (98.3%, a distinct unofficial-mock-problems benchmark, not the same test as this corpus's `aime` slug), FrontierMath, WeirdML v2, and Text Arena -- explicitly does NOT list MMLU, MATH, MATH-500, AIME, or HumanEval for this model. Scale AI SEAL leaderboard: search results confirm "The newest Claude models (4.7, 4.8, Fable 5) are not yet listed." LiveBench.ai and pricepertoken.com/leaderboards/benchmark/humaneval could not be read (JS-rendered / 403-blocked respectively) -- attempted but inconclusive rather than confirmed-absent for those two specifically.

5. Non-English sources: Chinese (Zhihu, CSDN, Tencent Cloud), Japanese (Qiita, note.com, several dedicated benchmark-guide blogs), and Korean (Elancer, Wikidocs, Brunch) coverage of the Opus 4.8 launch was searched. None report an MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval score for this model. Notably, one Japanese source (fyve.co.jp-linked search synthesis) explicitly observes that Anthropic deliberately omitted MMLU/GPQA-style "cleverness" benchmarks from official launch materials in favor of agentic/coding metrics, corroborating the round-1 finding from the System Card itself. A Korean source states the prior model Opus 4.5 scored MMLU 90.8%, but confirms no equivalent number exists for 4.8.

6. Sibling-model comparison tables: Checked GPT-5.5 and Gemini 3.1 Pro launch-comparison blog posts/tables that include Claude Opus 4.8 as a baseline. These consistently compare on SWE-bench Verified/Pro, Terminal-Bench 2.1, GPQA Diamond, ARC-AGI-2, HLE, BFCL, and OSWorld -- the industry has converged on this same "agentic/hard-reasoning" benchmark set instead of MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval, matching Anthropic's own System Card table. No sibling-model table was found carrying a classic MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval number for Opus 4.8.

Net: 6/6 new source types checked; zero credible, methodology-matched scores found for mmlu, math, aime, or humaneval. This is consistent with (not contradicting) the round-1 conclusion that Anthropic and the broader ecosystem have retired these four benchmarks as saturated/uninformative for this model generation, and that Opus 4.8 launched just after Stanford HELM (its most likely independent source) stopped evaluating new models.

2026-07-18 (round 3, archival/conference/investor/GitHub/analyst sweep for mmlu/math/aime/humaneval): Checked all 5 newly-specified source types (Wayback Machine, conference talks/podcasts, investor/press materials, GitHub SDK-repo issues, third-party analysts not yet checked). Found no credible score for any of the four gaps in this third independent round either. Conclusion: omitted_no_evidence stands for all four.

1. Wayback Machine: Used archive.org's availability API to confirm archived snapshots exist for the three key launch-adjacent pages: the Anthropic announcement (archived 2026-05-29, one day post-launch, http://web.archive.org/web/20260529001436/https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-8), the Vellum.ai "Benchmarks Explained" writeup this corpus already cites (archived 2026-06-11), and the BenchLM.ai model page (archived 2026-05-30). CDX-search for llm-stats.com's and artificialanalysis.ai's Opus-4-8 tracker pages from the launch window returned no snapshots in that timeframe. However, actually retrieving the archived HTML content was blocked throughout: web.archive.org returned HTTP 429 on every one of 10+ retry attempts across roughly 18 minutes of wall-clock time, using escalating backoff (up to a 150-second wait before the final retry) -- a persistent infra-level rate-limit, not a deliberate skip. Given that round 1 already fetched and exhaustively text-searched Anthropic's official System Card PDF directly (the authoritative source underlying Table 8.1.A) and found zero HumanEval occurrences, zero standalone MATH benchmark, AIME explicitly labeled "saturated" and dropped (p.198), and MMLU present only as GMMLU/MMLU-Pro variants that are not scored capability rows -- and given the corpus's own specSources already cite the live anthropic.com blog only for release date/pricing/fast-mode (never benchmarks) -- it is very unlikely an archived snapshot would surface a number the System Card itself never contained. This sub-check is best described as genuinely-attempted-but-rate-limited-inconclusive rather than a clean pass, though it does not change the overall conclusion given the corroboration below.

2. Conference talks / keynotes / podcasts: Mapped Anthropic's "Code with Claude 2026" tour -- San Francisco (May 6), London (May 19-21, before the May 28 Opus 4.8 launch, covered Opus 4.7/Mythos Preview only), and Tokyo (June 10-11, the one APAC keynote after launch). Checked session summaries and a Japanese-language live-blog recap (uravation.com) of the Tokyo/launch coverage: both discuss Online-Mind2Web (84%) and the ~4x reduction in unflagged code defects, but neither cites MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. Also checked Simon Willison's launch-day post (simonwillison.net/2026/May/28/claude-opus-4-8/), the "How I AI" podcast episode, and Dan Shipper/Every's "Vibe Check" episode -- none mention these four benchmarks with a number.

3. Investor / press materials: Anthropic is not publicly traded and has no earnings-call transcripts. It confidentially submitted a draft S-1 to the SEC on 2026-06-01 (targeting a ~$965B valuation, on the heels of a $65B Series H), but confidential S-1 filings are not publicly accessible, so no filing content could be checked for a benchmark citation. Public press coverage (Yahoo Finance, Sherwood News, SiliconANGLE, CNBC) of the funding round and IPO filing cites only revenue/valuation figures ($47B ARR run-rate, Q2 2026 revenue projections), never model benchmark scores. Ruled out: no public financial-disclosure document exists for this model that could carry a benchmark figure.

4. GitHub issues / SDK repo discussions: Searched anthropics/claude-code and anthropics/anthropic-sdk-python (issues, PRs, and GraphQL DISCUSSION search) for "claude-opus-4-8" combined with MMLU, MATH, AIME, and HumanEval. Zero relevant hits in either repo. A broader GitHub-wide code/issue search surfaced dozens of unrelated third-party repos merely bumping their hardcoded model-id string to "claude-opus-4-8" (routine SDK version bumps: aws-samples/bedrock-chat, jhcwalsh/Ben-job-search, etc.) -- none contain benchmark discussion, and no official Anthropic engineer reply with a benchmark number was found anywhere.

5. Third-party review/analysis sites not yet checked this session: Simon Willison (simonwillison.net), Zvi Mowshowitz's "Don't Worry About the Vase" (thezvi.wordpress.com/2026/05/29/claude-opus-4-8-the-system-card/ -- a post that normally reproduces system-card benchmark tables in detail and explicitly does NOT mention MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval, strong independent corroboration that Table 8.1.A genuinely excludes them), SemiAnalysis (newsletter + X coverage, cites only SWE-bench/Terminal-Bench/agentic metrics), CodeRabbit's release-day blog, llm-boss.com, ofox.ai, believemy.com, and requesty.ai. All either omit the four benchmarks entirely, or (requesty.ai) surface only a generic repeated FAQ sentence naming benchmark categories with zero attached scores -- confirmed by direct HTML inspection (curl) that the "MMLU Pro... MATH, AIME... HumanEval" phrase is identical boilerplate template text appearing 4 times verbatim on the page, not real per-benchmark data for this model. Believemy.com's reviewer explicitly notes "these figures come from Anthropic and have not yet been confirmed by independent evaluations" while confirming the same four benchmarks are absent from what Anthropic published.

Net: 5/5 new source types genuinely attempted (Wayback content-fetch was rate-limited but its availability-check plus the round-1 System Card finding make the negative result robust regardless); zero credible, methodology-matched scores found for mmlu, math, aime, or humaneval in this third consecutive independent round (round 1: official/tracker sources; round 2: academic/aggregator/regional sources; round 3: archival/conference/investor/GitHub/independent-analyst sources). This closes out the three-round research protocol: the absence of these four legacy benchmarks for Claude Opus 4.8 should now be treated as a stable, durable, and expected data gap rather than a target for further re-research.

2026-07-24 FRONTIERBENCH: ingested the FrontierBench v0.1 leaderboard row (see the result's own notes for harness, effort, CI, tokens and cost).
